sections.paragraph:
ἀνεκλάλητος, ον (ἀ-+ ἐκλαλέω q.v.; Diosc., Eup. preface Wellm.; Heliod. 6, 15, 4; Ps.-Callisth. 1, 40, 5; Herm Wr. 1, 4, 31; Eunap. 486 [Vi. Soph.. 10, 2, 1]; Iren. 1, 14, 5 [Harv. I 137, 11) inexpressible χαρᾷ ἀ. καὶ δεδοξασμένῃ 1 Pt 1:8; Pol 1:3. φῶς ἀ. (JosAs 14:3) of the radiance of the star at Jesus’ birth IEph 19:2.—DELG s.v. λαλέω B.

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

Eunap Eunap , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 76
